pattern: For each valid 3x3 top-left corner check all adjacent pixel pairs; if all differences are within threshold, the block is a region and each of its 9 pixels accumulates that region's floor-average.
hints: A 3x3 region is valid only if every horizontally or vertically adjacent pair of pixels within it has absolute difference at most threshold; there are 12 such pairs.
If a 3x3 subgrid is valid, compute its average (sum of 9 values divided by 9, floored) and add it to an accumulator for each of the 9 covered pixels.
For each pixel in the result, if it belonged to at least one region, output the floor of (sum of region averages) / (number of regions); otherwise copy the original pixel value.

edge_inputs.description: two stacked regions sharing middle rows
only one 3x3 block but it fails the threshold, image unchanged
threshold=255, every possible 3x3 is a valid region
single valid region in corner of larger grid

stress.description: medium random grayscale image with moderate threshold
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(3, size)
    n = max(3, size)
    image = [[rng.randint(0, 255) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    threshold = rng.randint(0, 30)
    return [image, threshold]
